data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:40:32.618Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

Let me recall what I know about this topic:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, there has been a progression of changes around synchronous operations:

1. In earlier versions, the Cosmos DB provider allowed sync operations by internally doing sync-over-async.
2. In .NET 8/EF Core 8, they started warning about this.
3. In .NET 10/EF Core 10, synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider were deprecated, but you could opt in to allow them via a configuration option.


4. In .NET 11/EF Core 11, the sync-over-async support would likely be fully removed, though I should note that as of my knowledge cutoff in April 2025, .NET 11 hasn't been released yet.

I'm trying to recall the exact timeline of when the Cosmos provider moved toward async-only operations. The provider has been gradually tightening its async requirements over several versions, and by the time we reach .NET 11, it's reasonable to expect that synchronous LINQ operations would no longer be supported at all. be fully removed in .NET 11, so synchronous operations on Cosmos DB will no longer work at all. The migration path is straightforward: convert all sync LINQ and SaveChanges calls to their async equivalents throughout the codebase.
